expectations.text: A markdown file was created inside a 'decisions' subdirectory (not just in the outputs root)
expectations.evidence: metrics.json shows files_created includes '.agent-docs/decisions/2026-03-03_164230Z_claude-sonnet-4-6_lucia-to-clerk-auth-decision.md', confirming the file was created in the decisions subdirectory of the real project

expectations.text: The filename includes a UTC timestamp in YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format
expectations.evidence: Filename is '2026-03-03_164230Z_claude-sonnet-4-6_lucia-to-clerk-auth-decision.md', which matches the YYYY-MM-DD_HHMMSSZ format (2026-03-03_164230Z)

expectations.text: The document has a section for options considered, covering both Lucia and Clerk
expectations.evidence: Document contains '## Options Considered' section with '### Option 1: Continue with Lucia Auth' and '### Option 2: Switch to Clerk' subsections

expectations.text: The document has a decision section clearly naming Clerk as the chosen option
expectations.evidence: Document contains '## Decision' section with 'The project will switch to Clerk.' as the opening statement

expectations.text: The document has a consequences or trade-offs section
expectations.evidence: Document contains '## Consequences' section with subsections '**Trade-offs accepted:**', '**Risks:**', and '**Triggers for revisiting this decision:**'

expectations.text: The document mentions session management or social logins as advantages of Clerk
expectations.evidence: Under Option 2 pros: 'Session management handled out of the box (token rotation, multi-session, device management)' and 'Social logins (Google, GitHub, Apple, etc.) provided without per-provider manual integration'. Also repeated in the Decision section.

expectations.text: The document mentions Lucia requiring too much manual work
expectations.evidence: Decision section states: 'The primary driver is that Lucia required too much manual work — particularly around session management and social login integrations.'

suggestions: Consider adding a timing.json file to the eval run directory so execution duration metrics can be captured

The eval prompt directly stated the advantages of Clerk and the problems with Lucia, so the skill had clear guidance; future evals could test with less explicit prompts to verify the skill's ability to infer and structure content more independently
eval_feedback.overall: The skill performed perfectly on all 8 assertions with a 100% pass rate. The document was correctly placed in the decisions subdirectory, had the proper filename format with UTC timestamp, correct frontmatter, well-structured options section covering both Lucia and Clerk, a clear decision naming Clerk, a consequences/trade-offs section, and explicitly mentioned both session management/social logins as Clerk advantages and Lucia requiring too much manual work. The skill produced a high-quality, well-structured architectural decision record.
